{{Quote|As long as Filry Vorru's people are willing to abide by the conduct standards we set for our law enforcement and militia forces, they will remain within the bounds of our trust. If they step outside those guidelines, they will be outside their lawful duty and will be dealt with in a suitable manner.|Mon Mothma|<ref name="KrytosTrap">''[[X-Wing: The Krytos Trap]]''</ref>}}
The '''Imperial Center People's Militia''' was a [[Militia/Legends|militia]] and [[Law enforcement agency/Legends|security force]] created to police the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|underworld]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] after its capture by the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. The militia was led by former [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Fliry Vorru]] who, while rejecting the position of [[Commissioner of the Coruscant Constabulary|Commissioner]], was given the rank of [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] in the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Constabulary]]. However, unofficially the militia was used by the former [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]], who controlled Vorru, to further her attempts to destroy the New Republic.

==History==
{{Quote|While we put much of our misfortune concerning the operations to take Coruscant down to having Zekka Thyne and other Imperial spies in our midst, it is entirely possible Vorru was working directly for Isard at that point. He certainly was in her employ by the time he was appointed a Colonel in the Coruscant Constabulary.|Winter Celchu providing a briefing on Vorru's activities|<ref name="BactaWar" />}}
Prior to the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] [[Liberation of Coruscant (Galactic Civil War)|capture]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], several persons, many who were former [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] members, were released by New Republic forces from the prison planet of [[Kessel/Legends|Kessel]] and smuggled into Coruscant. Former Imperial [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Fliry Vorru]] was sent in as well with as the New Republic hoped that he could revive Black Sun and cause enough to disruptions that would allow for the New Republic to take Coruscant. While the remnants of Black Sun were able to cause significant issues for the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Constabulary]] and [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] forces, Vorru was unable to fully re-establish the criminal syndicate. After Coruscant fell to the New Republic, they began the task of establishing a government.<ref name="KrytosTrap" />

However, they discovered the former [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]] had ordered the release of a deadly toxin called the [[Krytos virus]] infecting a large portion of Coruscant's non-[[Human/Legends|human]] population. The cure was found to be [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] however it was in short supply causing a huge demand for it on the black market. With law enforcement resources limited, Vorru offered to the [[New Republic Provisional Council]] to provide police services to the underworld and to control the black market. While the Council did not grant Vorru the position of [[Commissioner of the Coruscant Constabulary|Commissioner]],<ref name="KrytosTrap" /> he was given the rank of [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] in the [[Coruscant Security Force/Legends|Coruscant Constabulary]]<ref name="BactaWar" /> and allowed to form the Imperial Center People's Militia.<ref name="KrytosTrap" />

At the same time, the New Republic was attempting to counter the [[Terrorist/Legends|terrorist]] activities of former [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] [[Kirtan Loor]] who was leading the [[Palpatine Counter-Insurgency Front]]. Unknown to the New Republic, Vorru began a complicated game of allegiances, playing Ysanne Isard, [[Kirtan Loor]], and the New Republic against one another, including forcing Loor to take orders from him on which targets to hit and when. Despite his efforts to advance his own agenda, Vorru eventually became a pawn of Isard.<ref name="KrytosTrap" /> When Isard fled Coruscant on the ''[[Lusankya/Legends|Lusankya]]'', Vorru left with her leaving the People's Militia leaderless and fragmented.<ref name="CSWE" />
